summary: Round-1 findings T4-S1..T4-S5 are all addressed correctly. T4-S1: containment check uses canonicalPath + File.separator suffix, correctly defeating symlink, trailing-slash, sibling-prefix (attachments2) and equality-with-root edge cases on both Windows (test) and Android (prod). T4-S2: rename folded into withTransaction + outer catch removes the rename-fail-then-crash window cleanly; the snapshot-and-restore test seam faithfully models Room's rollback semantics. T4-S3/S4: documented in KDoc. T4-S5: acknowledged. One NEW minor issue introduced by the rename-inside-transaction fix: a process kill between a successful rename(2) and Room's WAL commit leaves an orphan attachments directory under filesDir/attachments/<id>/ with no matching chat row, which sweepZombieChats does NOT detect (it only sweeps row-orphans). Status: approved.

findings.issue: Folding rename into the Room transaction (T4-S2 fix) introduces a new narrow crash window in the opposite direction. POSIX rename(2) is atomic — once it returns true, the staging dir is gone and the directory exists at filesDir/attachments/{id}/. If the process is killed AFTER rename succeeds but BEFORE Room writes the SQLite COMMIT to WAL, the next app start will see: (a) no chat row (Room rolled back the autoincrement-allocated id on recovery) and (b) an orphan attachments directory under that id. sweepZombieChats walks ChatEntity rows and removes rows whose dir is missing — it does NOT walk the on-disk attachments tree to remove dirs whose row is missing. The orphan is invisible to the user (no chat list entry) but consumes disk space indefinitely. Bytes are app-private and contain only what the user staged, so no exfiltration risk; this is a robustness gap, not a security exploit.
findings.recommendation: Extend sweepZombieChats with a second pass that lists filesDir/attachments/, parses each subdirectory name as Long, and deletes any whose chat row does not exist (skip non-numeric names like '.staging-*' to avoid racing in-flight commits). Reuse ChatDao.getById(id) for the lookup. This mirrors the existing row-orphan sweep but in the opposite direction. Alternative (lower-effort): document the residual gap in a KDoc comment on commitDraftChat noting that mid-commit process kills can leak a directory but never a row — the inverse of the pre-fix gap.

findings.issue: renameTo is invoked inside database.withTransaction { ... }, which holds a SQLite writer lock for the duration. On a healthy Android device with staging on the same partition as the destination (enforced by the T4-S1 containment check), rename(2) is an inode-update of microseconds order, so writer-lock contention is negligible. But the lock-holding behavior is worth being aware of: any future change that allows staging on a different mount point, or that performs a copy/sync inside the same try block, would block all other DB writers (message inserts from the streaming UI) for the copy duration. Not exploitable, no current issue.
findings.recommendation: No code change required. Optional defense-in-depth: add a one-line KDoc note on commitDraftChat stating that the rename runs under the SQLite writer lock and therefore must remain a same-filesystem rename — which is already enforced by the containment check on stagingDir.

findings.issue: The test-seam transactionRunner in commitDraftChat_renameFailsAfterRoomInsert and commitDraftChat_messageInsertFails_chatRollbackToo snapshots chatDao.byId / messageDao.byChat and restores them on throw, but does NOT restore the fakes' internal MutableStateFlow `state` field, the `nextId` counter, or the insertCalls counters. The current assertions do not depend on those (only the byId map state is inspected post-rollback), so the tests are correct as written. However, a future test that adds an observeAll().collect { ... } assertion after a rollback would see stale flow values, and one that asserts on contiguous chat IDs after rollback would see an id gap. Neither is a security concern; flagging as a test-quality maintenance note.
findings.recommendation: Optional: in the test fakes, derive insertCalls/state from the byId map instead of tracking them separately, OR have the snapshot/restore helper also reassign state.value and nextId. No action required while existing assertions hold.
